tasks.name: Create Admin Topics Page
tasks.description: Implement the main admin topics page route at `/admin/topics`. This page will display a table of all topics with pagination, search functionality, and actions to create, edit, or delete topics. The implementation should follow the same patterns as other admin pages, particularly payments management.

relatedFiles.description: Main admin topics listing page with table and actions
Reference for admin page structure and patterns

tasks.implementationGuide:
1. Create `/src/app/[locale]/admin/(dashboard)/topics/page.tsx` with:
- Server component using getTranslations
- requireAdmin authentication check
- Supabase client initialization
- Direct query to fetch topics with ordering by creation date
- Client-side pagination similar to payments page
- Search implementation using topic name across languages
- Table with columns: Arabic name, Created date, Actions
- 'Create Topic' button that opens modal
- RTL support for Arabic locale

The component should reuse admin UI patterns and components such as Card, Button, and pagination from Flowbite.
tasks.verificationCriteria:
- Page loads and displays topics in a table
- Authentication prevents non-admin access
- Pagination works correctly
- Search filter functions properly
- Create button exists and opens modal
- UI is consistent with other admin pages
- RTL layout works correctly for Arabic locale
tasks.analysisResult: The Topic Management feature for Eventy360 will allow admins to create, edit, and delete topics that can be used by events and subscribed to by researchers. The implementation should maintain UI consistency with existing admin components and follow project patterns for Arabic language support and RTL display. Direct Supabase queries should be used where appropriate instead of creating new RPC functions, and all admin actions must be logged properly.
tasks.summary: Successfully implemented the Admin Topics page following the patterns in other admin pages. Created a table listing topics with proper sorting, pagination, search functionality, and RTL support. Added translations for Arabic. The page is fully functional and maintains the same UI patterns as other admin pages for consistency.

tasks.name: Implement Topic Search Filter
tasks.description: Create a search filter component specifically for topics, following the same patterns as other admin filters like StatusFilter. The component should allow filtering topics by name across all languages (Arabic, English, French) and maintain URL parameters for shareable filtered views.

relatedFiles.description: Search filter component for topics page

tasks.implementationGuide:
1. Create `/src/app/[locale]/admin/(dashboard)/topics/SearchFilter.tsx` client component:
- Accept props: activeSearch, locale, translations
- Use form with method="get" to maintain URL params
- Add hidden inputs for current pagination params
- Implement search input with HiSearch icon
- Ensure proper RTL support
- Style consistently with other filter components

Design should match existing filter components like StatusFilter in the payments section.
tasks.verificationCriteria:
- Component renders correctly
- Search maintains other URL parameters
- Proper RTL support for Arabic locale
- Consistent styling with other admin filters
- Search icon positioned correctly based on locale
- Clear button works when search has value
tasks.analysisResult: The Topic Management feature for Eventy360 will allow admins to create, edit, and delete topics that can be used by events and subscribed to by researchers. The implementation should maintain UI consistency with existing admin components and follow project patterns for Arabic language support and RTL display. Direct Supabase queries should be used where appropriate instead of creating new RPC functions, and all admin actions must be logged properly.
tasks.summary: Successfully implemented the Topic Search Filter component following the patterns in other admin components. The filter allows searching topics by name across all translations (Arabic, English, French) and maintains URL parameters for shareable filtered views. Added proper RTL support with correct icon positioning for Arabic and implemented a clear search button for better user experience.

tasks.name: Create Topic Modal Components
tasks.description: Implement modal components for creating and editing topics. The modals should include form fields for multilingual topic names (Arabic required, others optional), show a preview of the generated slug, and handle form validation and submission.

relatedFiles.description: Zod validation schema for topic forms

tasks.implementationGuide:
1. Create client components for topic modals:
- CreateTopicModal.tsx - For creating new topics
- EditTopicModal.tsx - For editing existing topics

Each modal should include:
- Modal wrapper using Flowbite Modal component
- Form with fields for Arabic, English, and French names
- Client-side validation with React Hook Form and Zod
- Display of auto-generated slug from Arabic name (read-only)
- Proper error handling and success messages
- RTL support for Arabic locale
- Submit and Cancel buttons

Implement common validation logic in a shared getTopicSchema function.
tasks.verificationCriteria:
- Modals open and close correctly
- Form validation works (Arabic name required)
- Slug generation from Arabic name functions correctly
- Submit button disabled until form is valid
- Success messages shown on completion
- Error messages shown for validation errors
- RTL layout correct for Arabic locale
- Both create and edit operations function properly
tasks.analysisResult: The Topic Management feature for Eventy360 will allow admins to create, edit, and delete topics that can be used by events and subscribed to by researchers. The implementation should maintain UI consistency with existing admin components and follow project patterns for Arabic language support and RTL display. Direct Supabase queries should be used where appropriate instead of creating new RPC functions, and all admin actions must be logged properly.
tasks.summary:
Successfully implemented topic modal components for creating and editing topics with proper multilingual support. The components include:
1. CreateTopicModal - For creating new topics with Arabic required and other languages optional
2. EditTopicModal - For editing existing topics with pre-populated fields
3. TopicSchema - Zod validation ensuring Arabic name is required with proper slug generation
4. TopicActionButtons - Client component to handle the modal state and actions

The implementation includes:
- RTL support for Arabic language
- Form validation with real-time slug generation
- Error handling with proper error messages
- Success notifications
- Integration with the admin_actions_log table
- Complete translations added to the Arabic language file

tasks.name: Implement Topic Database Operations
tasks.description: Implement client-side functions for creating, updating, and deleting topics using direct Supabase client queries. These functions should handle data validation, error handling, and proper logging to the admin_actions_log table.

relatedFiles.description: Utility functions for topic database operations

tasks.implementationGuide:
1. Create utility functions in `/src/utils/admin/topics.ts`:
- createTopic: Insert new topic with name_translations, generated slug
- updateTopic: Update existing topic, regenerate slug if Arabic name changed
- deleteTopic: Delete topic by ID
- generateSlug: Helper to create slugs from Arabic name
- logTopicAction: Log to admin_actions_log with appropriate action type

All functions should:
- Validate input data
- Use direct Supabase client queries
- Handle errors gracefully with useful error messages
- Log admin actions with appropriate details

Use transactions where appropriate to ensure data consistency.
tasks.verificationCriteria:
- createTopic successfully inserts new topic with correct data
- updateTopic correctly updates existing topic
- deleteTopic removes topic and cascades to related tables
- Validation prevents invalid data
- Proper error handling for DB constraints
- Admin actions are logged correctly
- Slugs are generated correctly from Arabic names
- Error messages are user-friendly
tasks.analysisResult: The Topic Management feature for Eventy360 will allow admins to create, edit, and delete topics that can be used by events and subscribed to by researchers. The implementation should maintain UI consistency with existing admin components and follow project patterns for Arabic language support and RTL display. Direct Supabase queries should be used where appropriate instead of creating new RPC functions, and all admin actions must be logged properly.
tasks.summary: Successfully implemented topic database operations with comprehensive utility functions for creating, updating, deleting, and checking topic usage. Includes proper error handling, data validation, and admin action logging. The implementation follows best practices with strongly typed parameters and return values.

tasks.name: Implement Topics RLS Policies
tasks.description: Create a database migration to implement Row Level Security (RLS) policies for the topics table. Policies should allow admins full CRUD access, while regular authenticated users should only have read access.

relatedFiles.description: Migration for topics table RLS policies

tasks.implementationGuide:
1. Create a new migration file in `/supabase/migrations/` with:
- Enable RLS on topics table if not already enabled
- Add policy for admins to have full CRUD access
- Add policy for authenticated users to have read-only access

The migration should follow the existing pattern for RLS policies and ensure proper security while maintaining required access levels.
tasks.verificationCriteria:
- RLS is enabled on topics table
- Admins can perform all CRUD operations
- Regular users can only read topics
- Non-authenticated users cannot access topics
- Policies are consistent with existing RLS patterns
- Migration applies successfully
tasks.analysisResult: The Topic Management feature for Eventy360 will allow admins to create, edit, and delete topics that can be used by events and subscribed to by researchers. The implementation should maintain UI consistency with existing admin components and follow project patterns for Arabic language support and RTL display. Direct Supabase queries should be used where appropriate instead of creating new RPC functions, and all admin actions must be logged properly.
tasks.summary: Created a migration file that implements RLS policies for the topics table, allowing admins full CRUD access and regular authenticated users read-only access, while restricting unauthenticated users from accessing topics at all. The implementation follows the project's established RLS patterns for consistency.

tasks.name: Create Reusable TopicSelector Component
tasks.description: Develop a reusable TopicSelector component that can be used in both admin contexts and event creation/editing. The component should support selecting single or multiple topics, with proper RTL support and consistent styling with the rest of the application.
tasks.implementationGuide:
1. Create `/src/components/ui/TopicSelector.tsx` with:
- Client component with React hooks
- Props for single/multiple selection modes
- Supabase client integration to fetch topics
- Support for initial selected value(s)
- Dropdown or multi-select UI using Flowbite components
- Full RTL support
- Loading state handling
- Error handling
- Callback for selection changes

The component should be highly reusable and maintain consistency with other form elements in the application.
tasks.verificationCriteria:
- Component renders correctly
- Loads topics from database
- Allows selecting single or multiple topics
- Handles initial values correctly
- Proper RTL support
- Loading and error states handled
- Callback fires with correct selected values
- Consistent styling with other UI components
tasks.analysisResult: The Topic Management feature for Eventy360 will allow admins to create, edit, and delete topics that can be used by events and subscribed to by researchers. The implementation should maintain UI consistency with existing admin components and follow project patterns for Arabic language support and RTL display. Direct Supabase queries should be used where appropriate instead of creating new RPC functions, and all admin actions must be logged properly.
tasks.summary: Implemented a reusable TopicSelector component that supports both single and multiple topic selection with RTL language support. The component integrates with Supabase to fetch topics, handles loading and error states, and provides callbacks for selection changes. The implementation follows the project's design patterns, uses the singleton Supabase client pattern, and properly handles internationalization.

tasks.name: Update Translation Files for Topic Management
tasks.description: Add all necessary translation keys for the topic management feature to the translation files. This includes page titles, button labels, form fields, validation messages, success/error notifications, and other UI text elements.
tasks.implementationGuide:
1. Update translation files in `/messages/` directory (ar.json, en.json, fr.json) with new keys:
- AdminTopics namespace with keys for page title, description, table headers, actions, filters
- TopicForm namespace with keys for form labels, placeholders, buttons, validation
- Success/error message keys
- Modal titles and content

Ensure Arabic translations are high quality and appropriate for the academic audience. Follow existing translation patterns for consistency.
tasks.verificationCriteria:
- All translation files updated with new keys
- Arabic translations are high quality and appropriate
- English and French translations are accurate
- Keys follow existing naming conventions
- No missing translations for UI elements
- RTL considerations addressed in text where needed
tasks.analysisResult: The Topic Management feature for Eventy360 will allow admins to create, edit, and delete topics that can be used by events and subscribed to by researchers. The implementation should maintain UI consistency with existing admin components and follow project patterns for Arabic language support and RTL display. Direct Supabase queries should be used where appropriate instead of creating new RPC functions, and all admin actions must be logged properly.
tasks.summary: Updated and created translation files for topic management. Enhanced the Arabic translations with additional keys for the TopicSelector component. Created comprehensive English and French translation files with all necessary keys for topic management, including page titles, form labels, error messages, and UI text following existing naming conventions and ensuring high-quality translations suitable for an academic audience.

tasks.name: Implement Delete Topic Confirmation
tasks.description: Create a confirmation dialog component for deleting topics, warning users about the cascading effect on event_topics and researcher_topic_subscriptions. This is essential to prevent accidental deletion of topics that may be in use across the platform.
tasks.implementationGuide:
1. Create `DeleteTopicConfirmation.tsx` client component with:
- Flowbite Modal for confirmation dialog
- Warning message about cascade effects
- Option to show usage count
- Danger-styled delete button
- Cancel option
- Loading state during deletion
- Success/error handling

Ensure the component follows existing UI patterns and provides clear feedback to administrators about the consequences of deletion.
tasks.verificationCriteria:
- Confirmation dialog appears before deletion
- Warning clearly explains cascade effects
- Delete and Cancel buttons function correctly
- Loading state shown during deletion
- Success message on completion
- Error handling for failed deletion
- UI consistent with other confirmation dialogs
tasks.analysisResult: The Topic Management feature for Eventy360 will allow admins to create, edit, and delete topics that can be used by events and subscribed to by researchers. The implementation should maintain UI consistency with existing admin components and follow project patterns for Arabic language support and RTL display. Direct Supabase queries should be used where appropriate instead of creating new RPC functions, and all admin actions must be logged properly.
tasks.summary: Implemented a comprehensive DeleteTopicConfirmation component that warns users about the cascading effects of deleting topics. The component fetches and displays usage data showing how many events and subscriptions are associated with the topic, provides clear warning messages, and includes proper loading states and error handling. Integrated the confirmation dialog with TopicActionButtons to ensure it appears when a user attempts to delete a topic.

tasks.name: Add Usage Count to Topics Table
tasks.description: Enhance the topics table to show how many events and researcher subscriptions are associated with each topic. This information helps administrators understand the impact of modifying or deleting topics.

relatedFiles.description: Add usage counts to topics table

tasks.implementationGuide:
1. Extend the topic database query in the topics page to include counts:
- Join with event_topics and count unique event_ids
- Join with researcher_topic_subscriptions and count unique profile_ids
- Return counts with topic data

2. Add columns to the topics table for:
- Events count
- Subscriptions count

Use this data to also enhance the delete confirmation dialog. Consider performance implications and optimize the queries appropriately.
tasks.verificationCriteria:
- Table displays event count for each topic
- Table displays subscription count for each topic
- Counts are accurate
- Performance remains good with larger datasets
- UI remains consistent and readable
- RTL layout maintained correctly
tasks.analysisResult: The Topic Management feature for Eventy360 will allow admins to create, edit, and delete topics that can be used by events and subscribed to by researchers. The implementation should maintain UI consistency with existing admin components and follow project patterns for Arabic language support and RTL display. Direct Supabase queries should be used where appropriate instead of creating new RPC functions, and all admin actions must be logged properly.
tasks.summary: Implemented a real-time usage count display in the topics admin table that shows the number of events and subscriptions associated with each topic. Created a TopicUsageCount client component that fetches usage data from the backend, displays a badge with the total count, and provides a tooltip showing detailed event and subscription counts. Also updated the UI to ensure proper RTL support and added all necessary translations to the Arabic language file.
